vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-913: Auto-detect expired Codex auth and spawn re-authentication flow

plan.title: Auto-detect expired Codex auth and spawn re-authentication flow
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: When Codex OAuth tokens expire or refresh tokens get burned, all gpt-5.4 agents fail with 503 auth_unavailable. Users have no visibility until agents start dying — the real cause is buried in cliproxy logs.
narratives.Proposal: Add proactive auth detection (JWT expiry + log monitoring), a dashboard banner with one-click re-auth via terminal panel (supporting both browser and device-auth for headless), spawn guardrails that block before wasting API credits, and auto-retry after successful re-authentication.

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/codex-auth.ts with an async checkCodexAuthStatus() function. It reads ~/.panopticon/cliproxy/auth/codex-primary.json, decodes the JWT access_token exp claim (reuse the existing decodeJwtPayload from cliproxy.ts — export it), and returns a status object. Extract the JWT decode helper from cliproxy.ts into an exported function so both modules can use it.

subItems.title: checkCodexAuthStatus() returns {status: 'valid', email, expiresAt} when token exp > now
checkCodexAuthStatus() returns {status: 'expired'} when token exp <= now
checkCodexAuthStatus() returns {status: 'missing'} when codex-primary.json doesn't exist
decodeJwtPayload is exported from cliproxy.ts and reused in codex-auth.ts (no duplication)

items.title: Add burned refresh token detection from CLIProxy logs

narrative.Action: Extend checkCodexAuthStatus() to also read the last ~50 lines of ~/.panopticon/cliproxy/cliproxy.log and search for the pattern 'refresh token has already been used'. If found (and the log line is recent — within last hour), override status to 'burned'. This is best-effort: if the log file doesn't exist or the pattern format changes, fall back gracefully to JWT-only detection.

subItems.title: Returns {status: 'burned'} when cliproxy.log contains 'refresh token has already been used' in last ~50 lines within the last hour
Falls back gracefully to JWT-only status when log file is missing or unreadable

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/routes/codex-auth.ts with a GET /api/settings/codex-auth endpoint that calls checkCodexAuthStatus() and returns the result as JSON. Mount it in server.ts. All I/O must be async (fs/promises). Response shape: {status, email?, expiresAt?, message?}.

subItems.title: GET /api/settings/codex-auth returns JSON with status field ('valid'|'expired'|'burned'|'missing')
Route is mounted in server.ts and accessible from the dashboard
No sync I/O (readFileSync, execSync) in the route handler

items.title: Add Codex auth check to spawn guardrails

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts, extend evaluateSpawnGuardrails() to check Codex auth status when the requested model routes to the codex provider. If expired/burned, block the spawn (429) with an actionable error message pointing to the re-auth flow. Also replace the sync isCliproxyRunning() call in the spawn path (agents.ts:948) with isCliproxyRunningAsync() which already exists at cliproxy.ts:401.

subItems.title: Agent spawn returns 429 with descriptive error when Codex auth is expired/burned and the model requires codex provider
Spawn proceeds normally for non-codex models regardless of codex auth state
isCliproxyRunning() sync call in spawn path replaced with isCliproxyRunningAsync()

narrative.Action: Add POST /api/settings/codex-reauth to codex-auth.ts. It spawns a tmux session named 'codex-reauth' running 'codex login' (or 'codex login --device-auth' if headless — detect via !DISPLAY && !WAYLAND_DISPLAY on server). Returns {sessionName, headless: boolean}. If a reauth session already exists and is alive, return it rather than spawning a duplicate.

subItems.title: POST /api/settings/codex-reauth spawns a tmux session running 'codex login'
Uses --device-auth flag when server has no DISPLAY or WAYLAND_DISPLAY
Returns existing session if codex-reauth tmux session is already alive (idempotent)

items.title: Add re-auth completion detection and token re-bridging

narrative.Action: Add POST /api/settings/codex-reauth/status that accepts the generated re-auth session name and status token in a JSON body, then checks if the codex-reauth tmux session has exited. When it has, call bridgeCodexAuthToCliproxy() to re-bridge the fresh tokens, then return {completed: true, authStatus: <fresh check>}. The frontend polls this endpoint after spawning the reauth session. bridgeCodexAuthToCliproxy() currently uses sync FS — create an async wrapper or use fs/promises for the server-reachable path.

subItems.title: POST /api/settings/codex-reauth/status returns {completed: false} while tmux session is alive
Returns {completed: true, authStatus} after session exits, with tokens re-bridged
No sync I/O in the completion check or re-bridge path

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CodexAuthBanner.tsx — a warning banner (similar pattern to StoppedAgentsBanner) that shows when Codex auth is expired/burned. Displays a message ('Codex authentication expired — gpt-5.4 agents will fail') with a 'Re-authenticate' button. The button calls POST /api/settings/codex-reauth, then opens the terminal panel for the returned session. Render it in App.tsx alongside existing banners.

subItems.title: Banner appears when codex auth status is 'expired' or 'burned'
Banner hides when codex auth status is 'valid' or 'missing'
Re-authenticate button spawns reauth session and opens terminal panel

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/hooks/useCodexAuthStatus.ts — a React Query hook that polls GET /api/settings/codex-auth every ~120 seconds. Returns the auth status for consumption by CodexAuthBanner and SettingsPage. Use refetchInterval with a reasonable default (~120s).

subItems.title: Hook polls GET /api/settings/codex-auth every ~120 seconds
Hook is consumed by both CodexAuthBanner and SettingsPage

items.title: Show Codex auth type and status in Settings page

narrative.Action: In SettingsPage.tsx, add a Codex/OpenAI Subscription section (or enhance the existing OpenAI ProviderCard) that shows: auth type is 'Subscription OAuth' (not API key), current status (Valid/Expired/Burned), email, expiry time, and a Re-authenticate button when expired. This makes it explicit that gpt-5.4 uses OAuth, not the API key.

subItems.title: Settings page shows 'Subscription OAuth' auth type for the Codex provider
Settings page shows live status (Valid/Expired) with email and expiry time
Re-authenticate button in settings triggers the same reauth flow as the banner

items.title: Auto-retry blocked spawn after successful re-auth

narrative.Action: In the frontend spawn flow: when a spawn is blocked by codex auth (429 with codex-auth error), hold the spawn parameters in component state. After the re-auth terminal completes (polling reauth/status returns completed with valid auth), automatically re-fire the POST /api/agents request with the same parameters. Show a toast: 'Codex re-authenticated — retrying agent spawn'. No server-side queue needed — the frontend holds the retry state.

subItems.title: When spawn fails due to codex auth, the original spawn parameters are retained in frontend state
After successful re-auth, the blocked spawn is automatically retried
User sees a toast notification about the automatic retry

items.title: Document Codex auth requirements and re-auth flow

narrative.Action: Update relevant documentation to explain that Codex subscription OAuth is required for gpt-5.4 model access, that tokens expire periodically requiring re-authentication, and how the dashboard re-auth flow works (including headless/device-auth). Update the cliproxy SKILL.md with the new dashboard-based re-auth flow as the primary path instead of manual codex login.

subItems.title: Documentation explains Codex is required for subscription (not API-key) mode
cliproxy SKILL.md updated with dashboard re-auth flow
